description:
Independent of fz-qbg. Latent silent-UB bug.

## Symptom

```fz
fn f(v) do
  with {:ok, x} <- v do x else :err -> 0 end
end
fn main(), do: print(f(:err))
```

Expected stdout: `0`.
Actual stdout: empty (sometimes SIGSEGV depending on heap layout).

## Diagnosis

`lower_pattern_bind`'s `Pattern::Tuple` case (`src/ir_lower.rs:1787`)
unconditionally emits `Prim::TupleField(subject, i)` for each element
WITHOUT first emitting a TypeTest to confirm `subject` is a tuple of
the expected arity. The codegen then emits `load notrap aligned vN+16`
on the subject — which for a non-tuple (e.g. an atom) reads heap
garbage. `notrap` suppresses the SIGSEGV; the program silently
continues with corrupted values, sometimes crashing later.

## Fix

The `Pattern::List` case at `:1794` already does this correctly using
`Prim::ListIsNil` + `Term::If` to gate head/tail projection. Apply
the same shape to `Pattern::Tuple`:

```rust
Pattern::Tuple(elems) => {
    let n = elems.len();
    let tuple_descr = crate::types::Descr::tuple_of(vec![crate::types::Descr::any(); n]);
    let test = ctx.let_(Prim::TypeTest(subject, Box::new(tuple_descr)));
    let project_b = ctx.cur_mut().block(vec![]);
    ctx.set_term(Term::If(test, project_b, fail_block));
    ctx.cur_block = Some(project_b);
    for (i, elem_pat) in elems.iter().enumerate() {
        let fv = ctx.let_(Prim::TupleField(subject, i as u32));
        lower_pattern_bind(ctx, fv, elem_pat, fail_block)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

(`Descr::tuple_of(vec![Descr::any(); n])` is "any tuple of arity N";
matches when the subject is a tuple with that many elements, fields
unconstrained.)

## What's removed

- The unguarded TupleField projection for non-tuple subjects.
- Silent garbage-read behavior; the `notrap` flag in codegen becomes
  provably unreachable from valid IR (could be tightened to `trap`
  later as a separate cleanup, but not in scope here).

## Acceptance

- Above repro plus the more complex `try_div` variant from fz-duq.4's
  investigation produce correct output.
- New fixture `fixtures/with_tuple_pattern_mismatch/` locks in the
  behavior across interp + JIT.
- All existing tests stay green (no fixture today uses tuple-pattern
  on non-tuple subjects, so no behavior change for existing programs).
title: lower_pattern_bind: TypeTest tuple before field projection
